content.seo_title: API Integration Guide: Build Your First Portfolio Project
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. Learn to master API integrations using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate guide for aspiring AI developers.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' problem, you must move beyond tutorials by building a live API-integrated application. By leveraging Antigravity for orchestration and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can ship a functional, data-driven project that demonstrates real-world engineering capability to recruiters and potential clients effectively.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of many promising careers. You’ve mastered the syntax and followed the tutorials, but without a tangible, API-integrated project, your skills remain theoretical in the eyes of hiring managers. The gap between 'student' and 'engineer' is bridged by the ability to connect disparate systems and make them talk to each other.

We are going to move you from passive learner to active builder. By integrating real-world APIs, you aren't just writing code; you are solving business problems. This guide will show you how to utilize Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development lifecycle, turning a blank slate into a portfolio-grade asset in record time.

steps.title: Define the API Architecture
steps.description: Identify a real-world problem, like a weather-based productivity app or a stock market sentiment tracker. Map out the endpoints you need before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior System Architect. Create a modular architecture diagram for a Node.js application that integrates the OpenWeatherMap API and OpenAI API to provide personalized daily advice.

steps.title: Configure Secure API Authentication
steps.description: Hardcoding keys is a rookie mistake. Learn to manage secrets using environment variables to ensure your application remains secure and professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure dotenv configuration pattern and a middleware function for Express that validates API keys via environment variables.

steps.title: Implement Data Fetching Logic
steps.description: Standardize your API requests using Axios or native Fetch with robust error handling to ensure your app doesn't crash when an external service times out.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a resilient API service class in TypeScript that handles retries, rate-limiting, and graceful failure for third-party API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the API service class with exponential backoff'

steps.title: Build the Transformation Layer
steps.description: Raw API data is rarely useful. Transform it into a format that your UI or secondary service can consume effectively.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a data mapper function that converts raw JSON responses from the weather API into a clean, simplified object for the frontend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a data mapper function for the weather response'

steps.title: Deploy and Document
steps.description: A project without a README is invisible. Document your API integration process clearly to demonstrate your technical communication skills.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md that highlights the API integration architecture, technical challenges solved, and instructions for running the project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a high-quality README.md including architecture diagrams'

pro_tips: Always wrap external API calls in a circuit breaker pattern to prevent cascading failures in your application.
Use mock servers for development to avoid hitting API rate limits and incurring costs during the testing phase.
Implement structured logging for all API traffic; it is the first thing an interviewer will ask about during a technical deep dive.
Version your API requests in your code so that if an external provider updates their schema, you can easily roll back or patch.
Focus on the 'Why' in your portfolio—explain not just that you integrated an API, but how it improved the user experience or business logic.
resources: MDN Web Docs on Fetch API: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Fetch_API
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is live, deploy it to a platform like Vercel or Railway. Then, write a 'Lessons Learned' blog post detailing one specific bug you encountered and how you debugged it using the tools learned here.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical roadmap to build a project that actually moves the needle on your resume. Stop waiting for the perfect moment and start writing the code that gets you hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Hardcoding keys is a rookie mistake. Learn to manage secrets using environment variables to ensure your application remains secure and professional.
Standardize your API requests using Axios or native Fetch with robust error handling to ensure your app doesn't crash when an external service times out.
Raw API data is rarely useful. Transform it into a format that your UI or secondary service can consume effectively.
A project without a README is invisible. Document your API integration process clearly to demonstrate your technical communication skills.
